name: Homemade Gnocchi
method: Boil, peel, and remove water from potatoes by running potatoes through a potato sieve. Some people bake the potatoes, remove the insides, and then put them through a potato sieve. It’s up to you and how much time you have. Spread the filling out and let it cool. When cool, make a little pile with the potatoes. Add one cup 00 flour on top of the potatoes. Make a small

hole in the flour and add an egg. Beat the egg into the flour. Now gather up the potato, and mix with the flour…I mash it all with my hands and gather more until it’s all combined. It helps to watch a tutorial video on how to make gnocchi. Once you get the hang of how much flour-potato ratio to use, it’s simple, it just takes a little practice. Divide the dough into 2 pieces…and roll out each piece to make a long rope. You can add flour if your dough is sticking. Cut 1-inch pieces down the rope to make the gnocchi. You can eat them just like this, or you can make lines with a gnocchi machine. Top with your favorite sauce! I learned to make gnocchi for this cookbook, as it has never been a big favorite of mine when I lived in Italy. We ate store-bought gnocchi whenever I made it at home, and it was okay, I guess. The first gnocchi I liked was at our favorite restaurant in Rome called Porto Corallo. That was where Leo and I had our first date which you can read all about in my first book, Confessions of an Illuminati Princess Volume 1. There is also Volume 2 if you would like to follow my journey and read about our incredible adventures in America traveling Route 66. In the next recipe, you will find the cabbage recipe that I tried to duplicate from that very expensive restaurant. Listen guys, I was successful on the first try, so check out my recipe coming up and give it a shot! It's delicious and easy! Never tell yourself you can't do something, give it a try and do your best. Serving suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Serves 2-3. Olé
